current.summary: Derived from syllago-docs session: canonical-keys.yaml only covers skills (13 keys). Holden flagged expanding to all content types as core scope, not optional. Work lives in syllago repo.

activeWork.description: Expand docs/spec/canonical-keys.yaml from skills-only to all content types: rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands. Then populate provider-format YAMLs with canonical_mappings for each new content type.
activeWork.progress: Nothing started. canonical-keys.yaml defines 13 skills keys. All 14 provider-format YAMLs have 6 content types each (skills, rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands), but canonical_mappings is empty ({}) for everything except skills.

recentDecisions: Canonical keys spec must cover all content types — rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands — not just skills. Holden explicitly said this is core scope, not optional (correcting an earlier dismissive take).
Provider-format YAMLs for all 14 providers already have the 6 content type sections (skills, rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands), but canonical_mappings: {} is empty for all non-skills types.
Loadouts do NOT appear in provider-format YAMLs — they are syllago-internal only. No cleanup needed here.
Drop 'extensions' framing — provider_extensions are provider-specific format details that haven't been mapped to canonical keys yet. This is a conceptual rename, not a schema change.

The syllago-docs site (capabilities pipeline Phase 2) already renders canonical_mappings from the YAML data. Once new keys are defined here and populated in provider YAMLs, the docs site auto-picks them up via _gencapabilities + sync-capabilities.ts.
nextSteps: Run /develop brainstorm in syllago repo: define what canonical keys exist for rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands. Use existing provider_extensions in provider-format YAMLs as a source of candidates — they represent provider-specific things that may warrant canonical keys.

For each content type, enumerate candidate canonical keys from the provider_extensions data across all 14 providers (e.g., rules: file_format, frontmatter_support, scope; hooks: event_model, execution_context; mcp: transport_types, config_location).
Update docs/spec/canonical-keys.yaml with new content type sections (matching skills section format: description + type per key).
Populate canonical_mappings in all 14 provider-format YAMLs for each new content type key — run capmon validate-format-doc after to check compliance.
Run _gencapabilities locally to verify capabilities.json includes the new mappings, then push so syllago-docs can consume them.

context.notes:
CURRENT CANONICAL-KEYS.YAML STATE:
  File: docs/spec/canonical-keys.yaml
  Covers: content_types.skills only
  13 keys defined:
    display_name (string), description (string), license (string),
    compatibility (object), metadata_map (object),
    disable_model_invocation (bool), user_invocable (bool),
    version (string), project_scope (bool), global_scope (bool),
    shared_scope (bool), canonical_filename (string), custom_filename (bool)
  Missing: content_types.rules, content_types.hooks, content_types.mcp,
    content_types.agents, content_types.commands

PROVIDER-FORMAT YAML STATE:
  14 files in docs/provider-formats/
  Each has 6 content type sections: skills, rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands
  Only skills has non-empty canonical_mappings (e.g., claude-code has display_name: supported)
  All other content types have canonical_mappings: {} (completely empty)
  Provider extensions per content type in claude-code.yaml:
    skills: (empty extensions — all mapped to canonical)
    rules: id=claude_md_files (CLAUDE.md convention)
    hooks: id=hook_events (list of events)
    mcp: id=mcp_transport_types
    agents: id=agent_file_format
    commands: id=command_file_format

CANONICAL KEY CANDIDATES (from provider_extensions across providers — brainstorm fodder):
  rules:
    - file_format (md, mdc, yaml, etc.)
    - frontmatter_support (bool — does provider read frontmatter?)
    - project_scope (bool — rules can be project-scoped)
    - global_scope (bool — rules can be global)
    - include_patterns (glob patterns for which files apply the rule)
    - enforce_mode (how the provider enforces rules: always/on-demand)
  hooks:
    - event_model (trigger-based, callback, lifecycle, etc.)
    - execution_context (before_tool/after_tool, session lifecycle, etc.)
    - shell_command (bool — runs shell commands)
    - timeout_support (bool)
    - parallel_execution (bool)
  mcp:
    - transport_types (stdio, sse, http)
    - config_location (where mcp config lives)
    - dynamic_registration (bool — hot-add servers without restart)
  agents:
    - agent_model_control (bool — can specify model per agent)
    - tool_restriction (bool — can restrict tool access per agent)
    - persistent_context (bool — maintains context across calls)
    - invocation_model (spawned/orchestrated/autonomous)
  commands:
    - slash_prefix (bool — uses /command syntax)
    - argument_support (bool — commands accept arguments)
    - output_format (plain text, structured, etc.)

CAPMON TOOLING NOTE:
  capmon validate-format-doc checks canonical_mappings keys against canonical-keys.yaml.
  Adding new content types to canonical-keys.yaml does NOT auto-fail existing YAMLs
  (capmon only validates keys present in the YAML against the spec, not absence of keys).
  This means: we can add canonical keys to the spec and populate provider YAMLs incrementally
  without breaking existing validation.

SYLLAGO-DOCS PIPELINE NOTE:
  _gencapabilities reads canonical_mappings from provider-format YAMLs and emits them as-is.
  Once canonical_mappings is populated for rules/hooks/etc., the docs site
  (CanonicalSupportTable.astro on canonical key pages) automatically renders the data
  — no code change needed in syllago-docs if the key names match.
  sync-capabilities.ts also reads canonical-keys.yaml from a relative path
  (../syllago/docs/spec/canonical-keys.yaml) to generate canonical-keys/*.json files.

RELATED OPEN EPIC:
  syllago-jtafb (in_progress): Capability monitor pipeline (capmon)
  Expanding canonical keys will generate new capmon work — new keys mean new
  monitoring targets for each provider. Coordinate with capmon epic after keys are defined.

nextSteps: Expand docs/spec/canonical-keys.yaml to rules, hooks, mcp, agents, commands
